kalkutta.note: Calcutta → Kolkata; Open-Meteo returns a Kerala village otherwise

seliger.note: Lake Seliger, Tverskaya oblast; geocoder returned a Svalbard point

ples.note: Плёс on the Volga, Ivanovo oblast; geocoder picked Plesetsk by population

rostov.note: Ростов Великий, Yaroslavl oblast — Ростов-на-Дону is the separate rostov-na-donu entry

echmiadzin.note: Echmiadzin (Vagharshapat); Soviet-era name still in common use

amalfi.note: Amalfi, Italy; geocoder returned a Colombian village

alaniya.note: Alanya, Turkey; alt spelling of Аланья

kemer.note: Kemer, Turkey; geocoder picked Kemerovo by population

datcha.note: Datça, Turkey; alt Cyrillic spelling of Дача

kash.note: Kaş, Turkey; geocoder picked a Chinese town

nisiros.note: Nisyros, Greek island in the Dodecanese

kos.note: Kos, Greek island; geocoder returned a Costa Rican village

gyullyuk.note: Güllük, Turkey; alt spelling broke matching

gyumyushlyuk.note: Gümüşlük, Turkey; fishing village near Bodrum

onezhskiy-ostrov.note: Bolshoy Klimetsky island in Lake Onega; ambiguous — Lebedev's Кижи is on the same island

lopskiy-ostrov.note: Лопский ostrov in the Onega Bay of the White Sea, near Беломорск

belomoro-baltiyskiy-kanal.note: Pinned to Повенец, the southern (Lake Onega) entrance of the canal

seldzhuk-efes.note: Selçuk, the modern town next to Ephesus ruins

bosdzhaada.note: Bozcaada (Tenedos), Turkish island in the Aegean

troya.note: Troy archaeological site, Turkey; geocoder picked Troy, Michigan

armutlu.note: Armutlu, Yalova, Turkey; geocoder picked the Armenian Armutlu

kordova.note: Córdoba, Spain; geocoder defaulted to the Argentine Córdoba

valensiya.note: Valencia, Spain; geocoder defaulted to the Venezuelan Valencia

ksantos.note: Xanthos, ancient Lycian city in Turkey

patara.note: Patara, ancient Lycian beach in Turkey; geocoder picked a Peruvian village

tartu.note: Tartu, Estonia; geocoder placed it in Syria
